name: GLM-5.2 (High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DSA + 'IndexShare' sparse-attention indexer reuse (2.9x fewer per-token FLOPs at 1M context), 1M-token context; selectable High/Max reasoning-effort modes
note: Default reasoning-effort tier: 'High' mode balances strong performance against latency/token cost for everyday coding, per Z.ai's own guidance. Same weights as the Max tier, selected via the reasoning_effort API parameter.
verificationNotes:
GLM-5.2 confirmed real and released via Hugging Face model card + blog (zai-org/GLM-5.2, huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog), zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub repo, official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2 (1M context, 128K max output, benchmark claims), and heavy independent coverage (VentureBeat, MarkTechPost, Pandaily, DataCamp, Together AI, Eigent.ai) of the June 2026 launch. Release date has conflicting reports: eigent.ai and Pandaily say June 13, 2026; docs.z.ai's own release-notes timeline and llm-stats.com say June 16/17 -- used June 13 as the more specific, earliest-cited date, but this is a genuine multi-day uncertainty. This entry represents the 'High' reasoning-effort tier. Z.ai's own MarkTechPost-covered launch explicitly did NOT publish a full official per-tier benchmark breakdown ('No Benchmarks at Launch' per that outlet's headline); the self-reported numbers that later appeared on the HF blog and in docs.z.ai are believed to represent the top (Max) configuration, so they are attached to the GLM-5.2 (Max) sibling entry rather than this one, and this entry's results are left empty to avoid fabricating a High-tier number that was never actually published. Sources also disagree on which tier is the true API default: eigent.ai's blog explicitly states 'High is the default mode ... Max raises the reasoning budget' (used here), but one WebSearch synthesis of another source claimed the opposite ('Max is the default and High is opt-in') and docs.z.ai's own code example shows reasoning_effort='max' in a sample call. Flagged as uncertain -- see 'uncertain' field. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Final diversified search for GLM-5.2 (High) (corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json) found ZERO citable benchmark results specific to the "High" reasoning-effort tier, confirming the entry should remain empty. Read existing verificationNotes first (already documented Z.ai's launch had no benchmark table, and later-published numbers were attributed to the Max sibling entry).

Searched this round: Chinese-language sources (智谱/GLM-5.2 跑分 via 36Kr, DataLearnerAI, Zhihu (知乎), 量子位/QbitAI, docs.bigmodel.cn official Chinese docs), OpenCompass/司南 (no GLM-5.2 entry found), SuperCLUE (no GLM-5.2 entry found), Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/glm-5-2 — page and Intelligence Index score of 51 / GDPval-AA 1524 Elo are explicitly labeled "GLM-5.2 (max)"), GitHub README (zai-org/GLM-5, fetched raw), Hugging Face blog, OpenRouter, Design Arena/LMArena-style leaderboards, llm-stats.com, BenchLM.ai.

Decisive finding: the official zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub README states verbatim: "`max` is the default... For default scenarios such as benchmark/leaderboard reproduction, keep `Max` (no setting required); only set `reasoning_effort=\"high\"` when you specifically want the `High` level." This confirms every published GLM-5.2 benchmark number (HLE, GPQA Diamond, AIME 2026, SWE-bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, GDPval-AA, AA Intelligence Index, etc., appearing on the official model card, Artificial Analysis, HF blog, and DataLearnerAI) reflects the Max configuration, not High — this is a structural reason (Z.ai doesn't benchmark High), not merely an absence of coverage. No vendor Chinese-language post, Chinese leaderboard, or independent outlet publishes a High-tier-specific number. z.ai/blog/glm-5.2 itself returned empty/JS-rendered content on fetch and could not be independently re-verified, but all secondary sources describing its table content treat it as a single unified (Max) score set.

Conclusion: no additions. This is the expected, honest outcome for a reasoning-effort variant whose vendor explicitly recommends and uses only the sibling (Max) tier for all public benchmark reporting. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specific structured sources requested for GLM-5.2 (High) (corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json) — no High-tier-specific results found in either, consistent with and reinforcing all prior rounds' finding that Z.ai only benchmarks/publishes the Max reasoning-effort configuration.

1. Epoch AI (https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv, both fetched directly): benchmarks.json is benchmark-definition metadata only (no model scores). benchmarks.csv is the real results feed (999 rows) and contains exactly one GLM-5.2 model id: "glm-5.2_max" (8 rows: EBR-bench 0.095, SWE-Bench Verified 0.787, OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025 0.864, GPQA Diamond 0.919, FrontierMath-Tiers-1-3-v2-Private 0.592, FrontierMath-Tier-4-v2-Private 0.293, SimpleQA Verified 0.381, Chess Puzzles 0.21; all dated 2026-06-16).
